2605.21071.title: Fine-grained Claim-level RAG Benchmark for Law
2605.21071.paper_summary: The rapid progress of large language models (LLMs) is shifting semantic search toward a question-answering paradigm, where users ask questions and LLMs generate responses. In high-stake domains such as law, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is commonly used to mitigate hallucinations in generated responses. Nonetheless, prior work shows that RAG systems, whether general-purpose or legal-specific, still hallucinate at varying rates, making fine-grained evaluation essential. Despite the need, existing evaluation frameworks for legal RAG systems lack the granularity required to provide detailed analysis of retrieval and generation performance separately. Moreover, current benchmarks are largely English-only and centered on legal expert queries, overlooking non-expert needs. We introduce ClaimRAG-LAW, a comprehensive dataset for legal RAG that supports French and English, targets both experts and non-experts, and includes diverse question types reflecting realistic scenarios. We further apply a fine-grained evaluation framework of state-of-the-art legal RAG systems, revealing limitations in retrieval, generation, and claim-level analysis in the legal domain.
2605.21071.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的快速進展正在將語義搜索轉向問答範式，使用者提出問題，而LLMs生成回應。<lb/>在法律等高風險領域，檢索增強生成（RAG）通常用於減少生成回應中的幻覺。<lb/>儘管如此，先前的研究顯示，無論是通用還是法律專用的RAG系統，仍然以不同的比率產生幻覺，因此進行細緻的評估變得至關重要。<lb/>儘管有這一需求，現有的法律RAG系統評估框架缺乏提供檢索和生成性能詳細分析所需的細緻度。<lb/>此外，目前的基準主要是英語為主，並集中於法律專家的查詢，忽略了非專家的需求。<lb/>我們推出了ClaimRAG-LAW，這是一個全面的法律RAG數據集，支持法語和英語，針對專家和非專家，並包含反映現實場景的多樣化問題類型。<lb/>我們進一步應用了一個細緻的評估框架，對最先進的法律RAG系統進行評估，揭示了在法律領域中檢索、生成和索賠級別分析的限制。
2605.21071.author: Souvick Das et.al.
2605.21071.authors: Souvick Das, Sallam Abualhaija, Domenico Bianculli
